Equity.question: What is DigiTrade A/c?
Equity.answer: It is a Trading cum Demat A/c

Equity.question: What are all the Charges to open DigiTrade A/c
Equity.answer: DigiTrade A/c is completely FREE, and we offer a complimentary Demat A/c with every DigiTrade A/c opened.

Equity.question: How to open a DigiTrade A/c?
Equity.answer: Your DigiTrade A/c [Trading cum Demat A/c] can be opened online in less than 10 minutes through our Mobile App/Web portal.

Equity.question: Documents needed for A/c opening
Equity.answer: PAN, Aadhaar, Bank Statement/Cheque Leaf, Your photograph through a Selfie & scanned Signature on your Mobile/Laptop/PC.

Equity.question: Steps to open the A/c
Equity.answer: Visit our Website, www.integratedindia.in, click on Open DigiTrade A/c and follow the steps further. Alternatively, you can download iInvest Mobile App & start your Journey in opening DigiTrade A/c

Equity.question: What is KYC?
Equity.answer: KYC is an acronym for “Know Your Customer” and is a term used for Customer Identification Process as a part of Client Onboarding with any financial entity.

Equity.question: What is Central KYC?
Equity.answer: Central KYC Registry (CKYCR) is a centralized repository of KYC records of customers in the financial sector through an entity substantially owned and controlled by Central Government to receive, store and safeguard the KYC records of a client in digital form.

Equity.question: How can I know about the trades executed in the Exchange?
Equity.answer: How can I know about the trades executed in the Exchange? - For the Trades done through offline, you will receive SMS on your registered mobile number & contract notes on your registered email ID with all the details of the transactions.

Equity.question: How to apply for IPO through online/mobile app?
Equity.answer: You can login to our Mobile App “iInvest” or web https://integrated.investments/login & apply for an IPO.

Equity.question: How do I get my user ID and password?
Equity.answer: Customer ID is the login ID. It will be communicated to you once the account is opened through email. Password will be sent through a separate mail.

Equity.question: How can I see account balance/statement online?
Equity.answer: To view or download the financial ledger and contract notes through Back-office, follow these steps: 1. Click on My Account. 2. Click on My Back-office → Show reports. 3. Click on Financial Statement → Refresh to view the statement and current balance. To download the statement, click on the 'Print' icon.

Equity.question: What are trading hours or market timings?
Equity.answer: For NSE Cash, BSE Cash, and NSE F&O, trading hours are from 9:15 am to 3:30 pm. Capital Markets operate from Monday to Friday and are closed on Saturdays and Sundays.

Equity.question: How can I check Bank, DP account details, and trades online?
Equity.answer: All the above information is available in your login. Use your User ID and secured password to access the same.

Equity.question: How to convert my trading account from offline to online and vice versa?
Equity.answer: You may contact our nearest branch office and request conversion from offline to online or vice versa.

Equity.question: How can I see/verify the fund transfer success status online?
Equity.answer: Log in to your account and select Fund Transfer/MF/Demat/NPS Payment report to see the transaction status. You can also verify this through Funds View - Funds transferred during the day in your online login.

Equity.question: How to withdraw funds/payout from my account online?
Equity.answer: Log in to our trading portal, select My Account, then Fund Withdrawal → Enter the amount → Click Request for withdrawal. Requests received before 4 pm will be processed the same day and credited to your bank account. Requests after 4 pm will be processed on the next working day.

Equity.question: Why is my payout/withdraw funds not processed?
Equity.answer: Reasons for rejection include insufficient balance, incomplete settlement process, or missing bank details (IFSC/MICR/Beneficiary).

Equity.question: Can I use the mobile app to withdraw/add funds?
Equity.answer: Yes, you can use the mobile app to withdraw or add funds to your trading account.

Equity.question: Can I issue a cheque/DD for my trades?
Equity.answer: Yes, you can issue cheques from your registered bank account, but exposure will be available only after cheque realization. DDs are not accepted as per compliance policy.

Equity.question: What is pledging of shares?
Equity.answer: You can initiate a pledge on the FREE holdings in your Demat account. Place a pledge request through your online login. You will receive a link from NSDL via your registered email ID and mobile number. Complete the process by filling your PAN number, confirming the scrip name and quantity, and authenticating with the OTP from NSDL.

Equity.question: Is it safe to pledge shares?
Equity.answer: Yes, pledged shares remain in your Demat account with a 'Pledged' status instead of 'Free'. It is as secure as other shares in your account.

Equity.question: When am I supposed to pay for my purchases?
Equity.answer: The settlement cycle is T+1 day. You must pay by T+1 day to receive shares in your Demat account. Delayed payments result in auto-pledge and additional charges. No payment by T+1+4 days will result in share liquidation by RMS on T+1+5 days.

Equity.question: How can I lodge my grievances/complaints?
Equity.answer: Send an email from your registered email ID to customercare@integratedindia.in. Our customer care executives will respond promptly.

Demat.question: What is a Demat account?
Demat.answer: A Demat account is an account that holds your shares and securities in electronic form.

Demat.question: Why are demat and trading accounts necessary?
Demat.answer: The trading account is used for placing buy/sell orders, while the Demat account serves as the one where your holdings are stored.

Demat.question: How does one open a demat account at Integrated?
Demat.answer: You can open a free demat account in India with Integrated by signing up on our website or the iInvest app and completing your KYC online in minutes.

Demat.question: Are my stocks holding safe?
Demat.answer: Yes. Your holdings are held with NSDL in safe custody, and they are under the regulatory ambit of SEBI.

Demat.question: Can I dematerialise any share certificate?
Demat.answer: You can dematerialise only those certificates that are already registered in your name and are in the list of securities admitted for dematerialisation at NSDL.

Demat.question: Do I have to pay any annual maintenance charges if I open my account through Integrated?
Demat.answer: No, there are no annual maintenance charges for opening an account through Integrated.

Demat.question: What is the meaning of 'nomination'?
Demat.answer: Nomination means appointing a person (nominee) who will receive the securities held in the demat account in the event of the account holder’s death.

Demat.question: Is there any minimum balance required in a Demat account?
Demat.answer: No, there is no minimum balance requirement. You can open and maintain a Demat account even if you do not hold any securities in it.

NPS.question: What is National Pension System?
NPS.answer: National Pension System (NPS) is a pension cum investment scheme launched by Government of India to provide old age security to Citizens of India. It brings an attractive long-term saving avenue to effectively plan your retirement through safe and regulated market-based return. The Scheme is regulated by Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA).

NPS.question: Who all are eligible to join NPS? (PFRDA)
NPS.answer: Any Indian Citizen (resident or non-resident) and Overseas Citizen of India (OCI)

NPS.question: Why should I open NPS Account?
answer: Below are salient features which make NPS different from others:
Low-cost produc
Tax breaks for Individuals, Employees and Employers
Attractive market linked returns
Easily portable
Professionally managed by experienced Pension Fund
Regulated by PFRDA, a regulator set up through an act of Parliament.

NPS.question: Can I open multiple NPS accounts?
NPS.answer: Yes, opening multiple NPS accounts for an individual is allowed under NPS. However, an Individual can have only one NPS account with each CRA’s and can have one account in Atal Pension Yojana, if he is a non-assessee.

NPS.question: Can I open an NPS account jointly with my spouse, child, relative, etc.?
NPS.answer: No, joint accounts are not allowed.

NPS.question: What are the types of accounts in NPS?
answer: Tier I & Tier II.
Tier-I is a Pension Account, which will have all the tax benefits under Income Tax Act.
Tier-II is an optional investment account available to a subscriber having an active Tier-I account. This account has no withdrawal restrictions and tax benefits.

NPS.question: What are the benefits of NPS?
answer: A Subscriber can contribute voluntarily at any point of time in a Financial Year
Subscribers can choose their own investment options, Pension Fund Manager, and see their money grow.
Portable - Subscribers can Port their account anywhere, even if they change the city and/or employment.
Offers multiple tax benefits for the contributions made by subscriber and his employer
No Long-Term Capital Gains on Exit

NPS.question: Who will invest my money in NPS?
NPS.answer: Pension Funds are responsible for investing contributions, accumulating them and managing pension corpus through various schemes under National Pension System in accordance with the provisions of the PFRDA Act.

NPS.question: What are the tax benefits under NPS?
answer: Tax Benefits to Individual: Any individual who is Subscriber of NPS can claim tax benefit under Sec 80 CCD (1) with in the overall ceiling of Rs.1.5 lac under Sec 80 CCE. Exclusive Tax Benefit to all NPS Subscribers U/s 80CCD (1B): An additional deduction for investment up to Rs.50,000 in NPS (Tier I account) is available under subsection 80CCD (1B) This is over and above the deduction of Rs.1.5 lakh available under section 80C of Income Tax Act, 1961.
Tax Benefits under the Corporate Sector:

answer.title: Tax Benefits under the Corporate Sector:
subPoints: U/s 80CCD (2) of Income Tax Act, employer's NPS contribution (for the benefit of employee) up to 14% & 10% of salary (Basic + DA) under New & Old Tax Regime respectively, is deductible from taxable income, up-to 7.5 Lakh.
Corporates: Employer’s Contribution towards NPS as per the above % can be deducted as ‘Business Expense’ from their Profit & Loss Account.

answer: Please note: Tax benefits are applicable for investments in Tier I account only.

NPS.question: What will be the investment proof to avail the tax benefit under NPS?
NPS.answer: The Subscriber can submit the Transaction Statement as an investment proof. Alternatively, Subscriber from "All Citizens of India" can also download the receipt of voluntary contribution made in Tier I account for the required financial year from NPS account log-in. It can be downloaded from the sub menu "Statement of Voluntary Contribution under National Pension System (NPS)" available under main menu "View" in NPS account log-in.

NPS.question: What are other tax benefits under NPS apart from available u/s 80CCD?
answer: Tax benefits on Partial withdrawal: Subscriber can partially withdraw from NPS Tier I account for specified purposes. Amount received from partial withdrawal are tax exempt u/s 10 (12B) of Income Tax Act.
Tax benefit on Annuity Purchase: Amount invested in the purchase of Annuity is fully exempt from tax. However, annuity income that you receive in the subsequent years will be subject to income tax.
Tax benefit on lump sum withdrawal or Systematic Lumpsum Withdrawal: Up to 80% of the total corpus withdrawn in a lump sum is exempt from Long Term Capital Gains Tax.

NPS.question: What are the tax benefits on investments under Tier II account?
NPS.answer: There is no tax benefit on investment towards Tier II NPS Account.

NPS.question: Where can I open NPS account?
NPS.answer: Applicant can contact any of our branches or visit our webpage https://www.integratedindia.in/NPS-Container.aspx?Open

NPS.question: Who is POP/POP-SP? What is the role of POP/POP-SP under NPS?
NPS.answer: PFRDA has appointed entities known as Points of Presence (POPs) to extend customer interface for all citizens of India, who wish to open an NPS account. POP is the first point of interaction between the subscriber and the NPS architecture. Point of Presence (POP) shall perform the functions relating to registration of subscribers, receiving contributions and instructions from subscribers and transmission of the same to designated NPS intermediaries. The authorized branches of POP for NPS services are called POP-SP (Points of Presence-Service Provider)

NPS.question: How can a Subscriber contribute to NPS?
NPS.answer: A Subscriber can make contribution digitally thro’ our APP / Portal. Alternatively, a subscriber can register for SIP thro’ our APP/Portal.

NPS.question: What are the minimum contribution criteria under NPS?
answer: A Subscriber is required to make an initial contribution (a minimum of Rs.500 for Tier I and a minimum of Rs.1000 for Tier II) at the time of registration. Subsequently, a Subscriber can make contributions subject to the following conditions:

answer.title: Tier I:
subPoints: Minimum contribution per Financial Year - Rs.1,000

NPS.question: When will the units be credited to my NPS account?
NPS.answer: POP, on receipt of contribution credited in its a/c by the payment gateway service provider, will ensure to credit the contribution to the Trustee bank’s a/c in T+1 day. Pension Fund Manager (PFM) will ensure investment of contributions received before 11 AM on the same day itself and contributions received after 11 AM shall be invested on the next working day. Units will be credited in the subscriber’s NPS account on the day, contribution is invested by the PFM.

NPS.question: How can a Subscriber change NPS account details?
answer: A Subscriber can request for change/correction in personal details, nomination details, bank details, reissue of I-PIN/T-PIN/PRAN Card to the POP-SP. A Subscriber can also update his/her photograph and signature by submitting a written request to the POP-SP.
Subscriber needs to submit the following forms for change request:
Form UOSS2 - for change in personal or nomination details or request for re-issue of T-PIN/I-PIN or Reprint of PRAN card.
Form S3 - Request for change in Scheme Preference or Switch or change of employment.
Form S7 - Request for change in Photograph and/or Signature

NPS.question: How can a Subscriber change his scheme preference?
answer: The Subscriber can change the scheme (asset class) preference through CRA log-in of their NPS a/c. You can follow the simple steps as given below to change the scheme preference online:

subPoints: Go to your NPS account log-in
Click on the sub menu "Change PFM-Asset Class" under the main menu "Make Transaction"
Select Tier type and change the Scheme preference as you intended to do.

NPS.question: How many nominees are allowed under NPS? Can a minor be a nominee?
NPS.answer: Subscriber is allowed to register up to three nominees in NPS. Yes, a minor can be a nominee. In such a case, the Subscriber will be required to provide the guardian's details and date of birth of the minor.

NPS.question: How to request for a duplicate PRAN card?
NPS.answer: In case of loss or damage of PRAN card, the Subscriber needs to submit a duly filled S2 form to the respective POP-SP. After verifying the form, the POP/POPSP will enter and authorize the request in the CRA system. Subsequently, the request is processed by CRA and a fresh PRAN Card is printed and sent to Subscriber's registered address. Alternatively, Subscriber can download ePRAN through their NPS account login.

NPS.question: How can I reset I PIN (generate password)?
answer: Subscriber can generate a password by following a few steps even without logging in to their account.
a. Visit the welcome page for NPS account login
b. Click on the "Forgot Password" link
c. Select "Instant Reset I-PIN"
d. Enter the minimum details required like PRAN, Date of Birth
e. Enter a new password of your choice
Subscriber should select any one of the following options:

I. Generate OTP: Once the Subscriber selects this option, a One Time Password (OTP) will be sent to the Subscriber's registered Mobile Number/email id. After receiving the same, the Subscriber needs to submit the OTP.

II. Go to Nodal Office: In this option, the Subscriber needs to print the acknowledgment and handover the same to any POP-SPs for authorization, irrespective of whether the Subscriber is associated with the POP-SP or not. After successful authorization by the POP-SP, the Subscriber can use the new password (provided during the initiation of the request) for login.

NPS.question: How can I get the Annual Account Statement for my PRAN?
answer: The Annual NPS Account Statement (as of March 31st of every year) is sent to the registered address of the Subscriber, if opted for Physical Statement at the time of registration. However, access to your Transaction is not only limited to annual statement. A Subscriber can download our Mobile App or access our Portal and can download holdings, recent contributions & transaction statement anytime (24/7) without any restrictions.
a. By accessing the NPS account through PRAN and password
b. Subscribers can also view Statement of Holding through account Log-in
c. Subscriber can also request for Transaction Statement through Integrated’s Mobile App.

NPS.question: Can I avail NPS related services online?
answer: Yes, certain services related to NPS can be availed online. These are as follows:
Change of address (correspondence and permanent)
Change of scheme preference
Change of mobile number and email
Online view of Statement of Holding (SOH)/Transaction (SOT)
ePRAN card view
Reprint of PRAN card

NPS.question: Where can a Subscriber register a Grievance / compliant?
answer: A Subscriber can register a grievance against any entity under NPS through logging into their account. Alternatively, they can visit the "Log Your Grievance/Enquiry" section under the "Subscriber's Corner" of this website to log the grievance/query. Through this platform, the Subscriber can register a grievance/query even without having the PRAN Details. On successful registration, a token number will be displayed on the screen for future reference.

In case a Subscriber needs to register a grievance against the POP-SP, they can send a mail to manikandan@integratedindia.in; babuk@integratedindia.in. The complaints/grievances will be resolved, and status will be updated within 1 working day.

NPS.question: How will the Subscriber know the status of the grievance lodged?
answer: When a Subscriber registers a grievance at the CRA website, a unique token number is assigned to each grievance. Subscriber can use that token no. to know about the status of the grievances either through the Call Center or through the CRA website. Please visit "Log Your Grievance / Enquiry" section under "Subscriber's Corner" to check the status of the grievance lodged.

NPS.question: What is the escalation mechanism for Grievance resolution?
answer: Escalation Matrix for Grievance redressal - Only for NPS, related to queries/complaints is given at the "Contact Us" section of this website. However, Subscriber must follow the process given in Question no. 1 of this section to get grievance registered. For details of Grievance Redressal Policy for Central Recordkeeping Agency under National Pension System, please visit "Grievance Redressal Policy" located at "Log Your Grievance / Enquiry" section under "Subscriber's Corner" of this website.

NPS.question: When can I withdraw my money from NPS Account?
answer: A subscriber can withdraw from NPS in the specific circumstances/conditions explained in the following query:

Partial Withdrawal - after completion of 3 years, a subscriber can withdraw 25% of his/her own contributions. A subscriber can withdraw up to 4 times in the entire tenure and every withdrawal should have an interval of 4 years.

Premature Exit - The subscriber may exit from NPS before attaining the age of 60 Years, at any point of time. In such a situation, atleast 80% of the Accumulated Pension Wealth of the subscriber needs to be utilized for purchase of annuity and the balance can be withdrawn as a lumpsum payment to the subscriber. In case, if the Accumulated Pension Wealth is < Rs.5.00 Lakhs, the subscriber may opt for 100% lumpsum withdrawal.

Normal Exit - If a subscriber joins NPS before 60 years of age and exits on completion of 60 years of age or on completion of 15 years, atleast 20% of the accumulated pension wealth of the subscriber must be utilized for purchase of annuity and the balance of 80% can be drawn as a lumpsum or in instalments. If the total corpus is < Rs.8.00 Lakhs, the subscriber may opt for 100% lumpsum withdrawal. If the Corpus is > Rs. 8 lakhs but, <Rs 12.00 Lakhs, then the subscriber shall have the option to withdraw an amount not exceeding Rs 6.00 Lakhs as lump sum and the balance of the Accumulated Pension Wealth shall be utilized to avail periodic payouts in the form of Systematic Unit Redemption for at least six years or Annuity or other options as may be approved by the Authority. However, the subscriber has the option to defer the lumpsum withdrawal till the age of 85 Years. Subscriber has also got the option to continue contributing up to the age of 85 Years.

If subscriber joins NPS after 60 years of age, then the Subscriber can exit at any point of time. The subscriber will be required to use atleast 20% of the corpus for purchase of annuity and the remaining corpus can be withdrawn as lumpsum or as Systematic Lumpsum Withdrawal. If the Accumulated Pension Wealth is less than or equal to Rs.12.00 Lakhs, the subscriber may opt for 100% lumpsum withdrawal.

NPS.question: What are all the permitted reasons to avail Partial Withdrawal?
answer: Partial Withdrawal is allowed only against the specified reasons such as,
Higher education of children
Marriage of children
For the purchase or construction of a residential house or flat as a one time withdrawal002C in his or her own name or in a joint name with his or her legally wedded spouse.
For medical treatment or hospitalisation of self or legally wedded spouse, children, including legally adopted children or parents.

NPS.question: Can I Pledge my NPS Corpus?
answer: Yes, Subscriber can seek financial assistance from a regulated financial institution and the lender may mark lien or charge on the individual pension account up to 25% of subscriber’s own contribution (i.e. within partial withdrawal limits).

NPS.question: Who will pay my pension?
answer: Presently, there are 15 Annuity Service Providers (ASPs) empanelled by PFRDA for providing pension. Upon attaining Superannuation, you can reach our team who will guide you further. For the list of ASPs and annuity variants, please visit our Portal / APP.

NPS.question: How will I receive my pension?
answer: Subscriber will receive pension from the Annuity Service Provider (ASP) according to the Annuity Plan chosen and purchased by the subscriber from the ASP as per the terms and conditions therein.

NPS.question: How do I decide the Annuity Service Provider (ASP) and plan for receiving pension?
answer: Presently the following (15) ASPs are empanelled with PFRDA for providing pension:
SBI Life Insurance Co. Ltd
Life Insurance Corporation of India
Star Union Dai-ichi Life Insurance Co. Ltd
ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Co. Ltd
IndiaFirst Life Insurance Co Ltd
Edelweiss Tokio Life Insurance Co. Ltd
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Co Ltd.
Canara HSBC Oriental bank of Commerce Life Insurance co Ltd
Kotak Mahindra Life Insurance Co Ltd.
Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited
Max Life Insurance Company Limited
PNB Metlife India Insurance Company Limited
Aditya Birla SunLife Insurance Company Limited
Shriram Life Insurance Co. Ltd
Following are the most common variants of annuity schemes that are available to NPS Subscribers:

Annuity for Life with Return of Purchase Price - Subscriber will get annuity for lifetime and on death of the Subscriber, payment of annuity ceases & 100% of the purchase price will be returned to the nominee(s)
Annuity for Life without Return of Purchase Price - Subscriber will get annuity for lifetime and on death of the Subscriber, payment of annuity ceases, and no further amount will be payable.

Joint Life Annuity with Return of Purchase Price - Subscriber will get annuity for lifetime and on death of the Subscriber, annuity will be payable to Spouse for lifetime. On death of the Spouse, payment of annuity ceases and 100% of the purchase price will be returned to the nominee(s)

Joint Life Annuity without Return of Purchase Price - Subscriber will get annuity for lifetime and on death of the Subscriber, annuity will be payable to Spouse for lifetime. On death of the Spouse, payment of annuity ceases, and no further amount will be payable.

NPS - Family Income Option with Return of Purchase Price - Subscriber will get annuity for lifetime and on death of the Subscriber, annuity will be payable to spouse of the Subscriber (if any) for lifetime. On death of Spouse, to dependent mother and then to dependent father of the Subscriber. On death of the last annuitant, payment of annuity ceases and 100% of the purchase price will be returned to the surviving children of the Subscriber and in absence of children, the legal heirs of the subscriber, as applicable.

NPS.question: How will I receive my pension?
answer: Subscriber will receive pension from the Annuity Service Provider (ASP) according to the Annuity Plan chosen and purchased by the subscriber from the ASP (Insurance Company) and the terms and conditions therein.

NPS.question: When and how the pension will get started for the Subscriber/Claimant?
answer: The annuity (pension) will be issued by the Annuity Service Provider (ASP)

On execution of Exit request in CRA, the annuitant’s details (Subscriber/Claimant details) and scanned documents will be shared with ASP as opted by Subscriber/Claimant. If the annuitant’s details and scanned documents are correct, ASP will issue annuity policy.
If documents uploaded are not sufficient/incorrect or any additional documents required, then ASP may contact Subscriber/Claimant for completion of annuity formalities.

On completion of annuity formalities, ASP will confirm Annuity request online in the CRA system and Funds (Annuity Corpus) will be transferred to ASP for issuance of annuity to Subscriber/Claimant within stipulated timeline.

NPS.question: Can Subscriber/Claimant change Annuity Service Provider or Annuity type after buying the Annuity?
answer: Once an Annuity is purchased, the option of cancellation or reinvestment with another Annuity Service Provider or in other Annuity scheme shall not be allowed unless the same is within the time limit specified (free look cancellation period as provided in terms of the Annuity contract or specifically provided by the IRDAI) by the Annuity Service Provider.

NPS.question: What happens if the Subscriber/Claimant dies after purchasing Annuity?
answer: The mode and manner of payment of amount (if any) will depend on the type of Annuity scheme selected by the Subscriber/Claimant while buying the Annuity. Family members of the deceased Annuitant need to contact concerned Annuity Service Provider.

NPS Vatsalya.question: What is NPS Vatsalya?
NPS Vatsalya.answer: NPS Vatsalya is a contributory pension system under the National Pension System (NPS). Its objective is to create a pensioned society and encourage the empowerment of children by inculcating the habit of saving for retirement from an early age.

NPS Vatsalya.question: Who can subscribe to NPS Vatsalya?
NPS Vatsalya.answer: NPS Vatsalya is open to all citizens of India who are under the age of eighteen years. The account will be opened and operated by the guardian on behalf of the minor.

NPS Vatsalya.question: What are the benefits of opening a NPS Vatsalya account?
NPS Vatsalya.answer: Opening a NPS Vatsalya account provides the child with a head start on saving for retirement and offers valuable financial lessons from an early age. It instils the importance of financial planning and discipline, which can benefit the child throughout their life.

NPS Vatsalya.question: How is the NPS Vatsalya account operated?
answer: The account is opened by the natural or legal guardian in the name of the minor.
The minor is the sole beneficiary of the account.
A unique Permanent Retirement Account Number (PRAN) is issued in the minor's name.
The account is operated by the guardian for the exclusive benefit of the minor until they reach the age of majority (18 years).

NPS Vatsalya.question: What are the KYC requirements for opening a NPS Vatsalya account?
answer: The KYC norms applicable to the guardian must be in accordance with the standards stipulated by PFRDA.
In the case of a court appointed legal guardian, a copy of the court order regarding the appointment of the Legal Guardian must be submitted along with the KYC documents.

NPS Vatsalya.question: What documents of the minor are required for opening a NPS Vatsalya account?
answer: For the minor, the proof of date of birth is required. Acceptable documents include:
Birth certificate of the minor
School leaving certificate / Matriculations issued by Higher Secondary Board of respective states, ICSE, CBSE, etc.
Passport of the minor
PAN.

NPS Vatsalya.question: Is a bank account required for opening a NPS Vatsalya account?
answer: The bank account details of the minor or a joint account with the minor are not mandatory for opening the account for Indian residents but will be required at the time of partial withdrawal or exit before the age of 18.
For non-residents, details of NRE or NRO account are mandatory.

NPS Vatsalya.question: What happens when the minor attains the age of 18 years?
answer: The account will continue to be operational and will be seamlessly transitioned into a NPS - Tier 1 Account under the All Citizen Model.
Upon transitioning, the features, benefits, and exit norms of the NPS-Tier I for All Citizen Model will apply.
A fresh KYC of the subscriber must be carried out within three months of reaching majority. Contributions to the NPS Tier1 Account will be allowed after the submission of fresh KYC.

NPS Vatsalya.question: Can an NRI or OCI open a NPS Vatsalya account?
answer: The minor must be a citizen of India.
The guardian can be a Non-Resident Indian (NRI) or Overseas Citizen of India (OCI).
A separate form is applicable for guardians who are NRIs or OCIs.
A bank account (NRE or NRO) is mandatory when the guardian is an NRI or OCI.

NPS Vatsalya.question: Is it required to furnish a nomination while joining the scheme?
NPS Vatsalya.answer: No, the guardian becomes the nominee under the scheme.

NPS Vatsalya.question: How many NPS Vatsalya accounts can I open?
NPS Vatsalya.answer: The guardian can open a single account (per child) for the minor.

NPS Vatsalya.question: Can a guardian who is a NPS subscriber open a NPS Vatsalya account for a minor?
NPS Vatsalya.answer: Yes, a guardian who is a NPS subscriber can open a NPS Vatsalya account in the name of the minor.

NPS Vatsalya.question: What are the minimum and maximum contributions under the NPS Vatsalya account?
answer: The minimum contribution is Rs 1000 per annum, with no upper limit on the maximum contribution.
The initial contribution required for enrolment under the scheme is Rs 1000.

NPS Vatsalya.question: What choices do I need to exercise under NPS Vatsalya?
answer: All choices available under the NPS All Citizen model are also available for NPS Vatsalya, including:
Choice of CRA (Central Recordkeeping Agency): From the registered CRAs with PFRDA.
Choice of Pension Fund (PF): From the registered PFs with PFRDA
Choice of Allocation of Funds:
Auto Choice:
Conservative Life Cycle Fund (LC25)
Moderate Life Cycle Fund (LC50) – Default
Aggressive Life Cycle Fund (LC75)
Active Choice:
Equity (E) – Maximum 75%
Corporate Bonds (C) – Up to 100%
Government Securities (G) – Up to 100%
Alternate Assets (A) – Maximum 5%

NPS Vatsalya.question: How are the contributions invested in NPS Vatsalya?
answer: The contributions made by the subscriber are invested according to the choices (Pension Fund and Asset allocation) exercised and recorded with CRA, in line with the investment guidelines prescribed by PFRDA for each asset class:
Asset Class E – Equity shares of Top 200 companies under NSE/BSE in terms of market capitalisation
Asset Class C – Corporate Bonds/Debentures that are listed and rated not below A,
Asset Class G – Government securities and State Development Loans,
Asset Class A – Alternate Assets. For detailed investment guidelines, refer to the Circulars Section of the PFRDA website.

NPS Vatsalya.question: How do I know about the performance of my NPS investments?
NPS Vatsalya.answer: The performance of your NPS investments is available in the Statement of Transactions, which can be accessed online through the subscriber web login or mobile app. Periodic statements are sent by the CRA to the registered email ID of the subscriber, and a physical statement for the financial year is sent to the correspondence address of the subscriber.

NPS Vatsalya.question: What happens if I don't make the minimum contribution? Will my account be closed?
NPS Vatsalya.answer: If the minimum contribution is not received, the account is categorized as 'frozen' and will be activated upon contributing to the account. The NPS account will be closed only when a subscriber submits a request (physical or online) for exit from NPS to a service provider (PoP).

NPS Vatsalya.question: Can I partially withdraw money from the NPS Vatsalya account before 18 years?
answer: Partial withdrawals from your NPS Vatsalya account are allowed to address contingency situations. The reasons/conditions for partial withdrawal include:
Education of the minor subscriber
Treatment of specified illnesses of the minor subscriber
Disability of more than 75% of the minor subscriber.
A maximum amount of up to 25% of contributions (excluding returns) can be partially withdrawn.
This facility is available on a declaration basis after a minimum of 3 years from the date of account opening.
The Partial withdrawal can be made maximum three times till subscriber attains 18 years of age.

NPS Vatsalya.question: What are the exit options under the scheme?
answer: The subscriber can exit on attainment of age of 18 years.
On such exit, at least eighty percentage of accumulated corpus available in the account must be utilized for purchase of annuity and remaining balance shall be paid in lump sum.

In case, the accumulated pension wealth available in the account is equal to or less than a two lakh fifty thousand, or purchase of annuity is not available from empanelled Annuity Service Providers ('ASPs'), the subscriber shall have option to withdraw the entire accumulated pension wealth.

NPS Vatsalya.question: What happens in case of death before 18 years?
answer: In the event of the minor subscriber's death, the entire accumulated corpus to be paid to the guardian.

If the guardian registered under the account dies during the account's subsistence, another guardian must be registered on behalf of the minor subscriber by submitting the KYC documents as specified by the PFRDA from time to time.

In case of death of both the parent, the legally appointed guardian can continue the account with or without making contributions to the account, and upon attainment of 18 years of age, the subscriber can exit from the scheme.

NPS Vatsalya.question: Whom should I approach if I have a complaint /grievance?
answer: For resolving subscriber grievances, the Authority has notified the PFRDA (Redressal of Subscriber Grievance) Regulations, 2015 and an online platform ‘Central Grievance Management System (CGMS)’ has been hosted for subscriber to lodge grievance online by logging to his/her NPS account.
A complaint/grievance has to be resolved by the intermediary concerned as early as possible within a maximum period of 30 days of the receipt of the complaint.
If a subscriber is not satisfied with the resolution provided, he/she can escalate his grievance to the next higher level for resolution and the escalation matrix is as under:

Insurance.question: What is health insurance?
Insurance.answer: Health insurance is a type of insurance that covers the cost of medical and surgical expenses incurred by the insured individual. It helps individuals to manage the cost of healthcare expenses and provides financial protection against medical emergencies.

Insurance.question: What are the benefits of having health insurance in India?
Insurance.answer: Health insurance offers several benefits, such as coverage of medical expenses incurred during hospitalization, coverage for pre and post-hospitalization expenses, cashless hospitalization facility, and tax benefits under Section 80D of the Income Tax Act, 1961 (available only if the taxpayer opts for the old tax regime)

Insurance.question: What are the types of health insurance plans available in India?
Insurance.answer: The types of health insurance plans available in India include individual health insurance, family health insurance, group health insurance, critical illness insurance, and personal accident insurance.

Insurance.question: What should I consider before buying health insurance in India?
Insurance.answer: Some of the factors to consider before buying health insurance in India are coverage and benefits, premium and deductibles, network of hospitals and healthcare providers, claim settlement ratio of the insurer, and customer service and support..

Insurance.question: How do I choose the right health insurance provider in India?
Insurance.answer: When choosing a health insurance provider in India, consider factors such as the company's reputation, financial stability, claim settlement ratio, customer service, and the types of policies offered. It's also important to compare the features and benefits of different policies to find the one that best meets your needs.

Financial.question: What are financial consulting firms in India?
Financial.answer: Financial consulting firms are professional service firms that offer expert advice and guidance on financial matters such as investments, tax planning, and risk management.

Financial.question: What are financial advisory companies in India?
Financial.answer: Financial advisory companies provide expert advice and guidance on financial matters such as investment strategies, retirement planning, and wealth management.

Financial.question: What should I look for in the best financial advisory services in India?
Financial.answer: The best financial advisory services in India should have a team of experienced and qualified professionals, a strong track record of delivering quality services, transparent fee structure, good client reviews, and a customer-centric approach.

Financial.question: How can financial advisory and consultancy services help me with retirement planning?
Financial.answer: Financial advisory and consultancy services can help you with retirement planning by offering expert advice on retirement savings, investment strategies, and income generation during retirement.

Financial.question: How can the best investment advisory firms in India help me with my investments?
Financial.answer: Investment advisory firms in India can help you with your investments by offering expert advice and guidance on which investments to choose based on your risk tolerance, financial goals, and market conditions.

Mutual Fund.question: What are mutual funds?
Mutual Fund.answer: Pooled investments managed by professionals that invest in stocks, bonds, or other assets.

Mutual Fund.question: What is an SIP?
Mutual Fund.answer: The Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) is an investment strategy in which a mutual fund scheme is selected and a fixed sum is invested at regular intervals.

Mutual Fund.question: Can I use the iInvest app to make online investments?
Mutual Fund.answer: Yes, you can use the iInvest app to invest in mutual funds and set up SIPs.

Mutual Fund.question: Do lock-in periods exist?
Mutual Fund.answer: Only for certain funds, such as ELSS (3 years). The majority of funds permit withdrawals at any time.

Mutual Fund.question: What is folio number?
Mutual Fund.answer: Similar to a bank account number, a folio number shows your assets in fund house schemes. With a fund house, there is no cap on the quantity of folios that can be held. However, having a small number of folios is an excellent choice because it makes tracking your investments easier.

Mutual Fund.question: What are the best mutual funds for long term growth?
Mutual Fund.answer: Funds that invest in diversified equity portfolios with a strong track record are often considered among the best mutual funds for long term growth. Always align your fund choice with your investment goals and risk appetite.

Mutual Fund.question: Are returns guaranteed in mutual funds?
Mutual Fund.answer: No, returns on mutual funds can fluctuate and are correlated with the market.

Mutual Fund.question: Can I stop or pause my SIP anytime?
Mutual Fund.answer: Yes, most mutual fund SIPs can be paused, stopped, or modified anytime through the platform or app you used to start the investment. There is usually no penalty for stopping an SIP.

GIP Strategy.question: What is the Growth, Income, and Protection (GIP) strategy?
GIP Strategy.answer: The GIP strategy is a holistic investment approach that aims to address various financial needs and goals of investors. It emphasizes allocating assets into three main categories: Growth assets, Income-generating assets, and Protection assets.

GIP Strategy.question: Why is it important to follow the GIP strategy?
GIP Strategy.answer: Following the GIP strategy helps investors achieve a balanced and diversified portfolio that caters to their short-term needs, long-term wealth creation, and financial security for themselves and their families.

GIP Strategy.question: What are Growth assets?
GIP Strategy.answer: Growth assets are investments such as stocks, mutual funds, or ETFs, which have the potential to provide capital appreciation over the long term. These assets aim to beat inflation and build wealth over time.

GIP Strategy.question: What are Income-generating assets?
GIP Strategy.answer: Income-generating assets include bonds, fixed income securities, hybrid mutual funds which provide regular income to investors. These assets are crucial for meeting short-term financial requirements and providing stability to the portfolio.

GIP Strategy.question: Why do we need Income-generating assets?
GIP Strategy.answer: Income-generating assets offer a source of regular cash flow, which can be utilized for daily expenses, emergencies, or planned expenses without having to sell growth assets during unfavorable market conditions.

GIP Strategy.question: What is the significance of Protection assets?
GIP Strategy.answer: Protection assets refer to insurance products such as life insurance and health insurance. They play a vital role in securing the financial future of investors and their families by providing a safety net against unforeseen events such as illness, disability, or death.

GIP Strategy.question: How much of my portfolio should be allocated to each category?
GIP Strategy.answer: The allocation depends on individual factors such as age, risk appetite, and investment horizon. However, a typical recommendation might be to allocate a significant portion to Growth assets for long-term wealth creation, a portion to Income-generating assets for short-term needs, and ensuring adequate coverage with Protection assets.

GIP Strategy.question: What if my financial situation changes?
GIP Strategy.answer: It's important to regularly review and adjust your portfolio according to changes in your financial situation, goals, or market conditions. Our advisors can help you reassess your portfolio and make necessary adjustments to maintain alignment with your financial objectives.

GIP Strategy.question: Can I customize my GIP strategy based on my preferences?
GIP Strategy.answer: Yes, the GIP strategy is flexible and can be tailored to suit your specific requirements and preferences. Our advisors can work with you to create a personalized investment plan that aligns with your goals and risk tolerance.

GIP Strategy.question: How do I get started with implementing the GIP strategy?
GIP Strategy.answer: Simply schedule a consultation with one of our financial advisors. They will assess your financial situation, understand your goals and preferences, and help you design a customized GIP strategy that suits your needs and objectives.

Fixed Deposit.question: What is Fixed Income?
Fixed Deposit.answer: Fixed income refers to those investment securities that give investors regular, fixed interest payments, and return the original amount of the investment at maturity. Unlike stocks or variable-income securities, fixed-income products have predictable payments which do not change during their period. Examples include government and corporate bonds and certificates of deposits (CDs).

Fixed Deposit.question: Reasons for Investing in Fixed Asset
Fixed Deposit.answer: For risk-averse investors consuming fixed return are the most appropriate. Regular income streams can be relied upon by investors wishing to smoothen their cash flows or conserve money. Such packages are better than shares when it comes to safety hence serve as better tools for spreading investment risk.

Fixed Deposit.question: Who Should Make Fixed Deposit Plans to Invest?
Fixed Deposit.answer: Retirees or retirees-to-be looking for consistent income should invest in fixed income as it offers lower risk in terms of a risk-return relationship. Basic portfolio stability with the help of the best bond investment platform is guaranteed through counterbalancing these securities with highly volatile ones like equities.

Fixed Deposit.question: What is a corporate fixed deposit?
Fixed Deposit.answer: A corporate (company) fixed deposit is a deposit placed by investors with a company for a fixed term. It carries a prescribed rate of interest.

Fixed Deposit.question: Is there any scope for the principal to appreciate?
Fixed Deposit.answer: No, at the end of the deposit period, the principal (original amount of investment) is returned to the deposit holder.

Fixed Deposit.question: How are interest payments made?
Fixed Deposit.answer: Interest on corporate fixed deposits is paid on a monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, yearly, or maturity basis. It is paid either through cheque or through the Electronic Clearing System (ECS).

Fixed Deposit.question: When is TDS deducted on the interest earned from corporate fixed deposits?
Fixed Deposit.answer: Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) is deducted if the interest earned on a corporate fixed deposit exceeds Rs.5,000 in a financial year.

Fixed Deposit.question: What is Form 15G/15H? Where can I access it?
Fixed Deposit.answer: Form 15G/15H is a self-declaration form that can be submitted by the depositor to avoid Tax Deduction at Source (TDS) if their interest income from corporate fixed deposits is higher than Rs.5,000, but their total income is below the taxable limit. This form does not require attestation, except in the case of a left-hand thumb impression, which must be attested by a gazetted officer/bank official. Form 15G/15H can be accessed from the ‘Downloads’ section of your account.

Fixed Deposit.question: What is the difference between Form 15G and Form 15H?
Fixed Deposit.answer: Form 15G is meant for resident individuals who are below 65 years of age. Form 15H is meant for senior citizens who are 65 years of age or more during that financial year.

Fixed Deposit.question: On the submission of Form 15G/15H, will there be any query from the income tax department?
Fixed Deposit.answer: Since one copy of the Form 15G/15H is required to be sent to the income tax department, it is possible that the department may raise a query if they deem it necessary.

Fixed Deposit.question: What is the difference between a fixed deposit and a cumulative deposit?
Fixed Deposit.answer: In a fixed deposit scheme, the interest is payable at specified frequencies, which is convenient for individuals like pensioners requiring periodic interest payments. In a cumulative deposit scheme, the interest is payable at the time of maturity along with the principal. This scheme is suitable for individuals not requiring periodic interest payments and seeking money multiplier schemes.

Fixed Deposit.question: What is the investment period for corporate fixed deposits?
Fixed Deposit.answer: Manufacturing companies can accept fixed deposits for a duration of 6 months to 3 years. Non-Banking Finance Companies can accept deposits for a duration of 1 year to 5 years. Housing Finance Companies can accept deposits for a period of 1 year to 7 years.

Fixed Deposit.question: What is the maximum amount of investment that a company can accept in a corporate fixed deposit?
Fixed Deposit.answer:
A Non-Banking Non-Finance Company (Manufacturing Company) can accept deposits subject to the following limits: 
- Up to 10% of the aggregate of paid-up share capital and free reserves if the deposits are from shareholders or are guaranteed by the directors. 
- Up to 25% of the aggregate of paid-up share capital and free reserves. 
A Non-Banking Finance Company can accept deposits up to the following limits: 
- An equipment leasing company can accept four times its net owned fund. 
- A loan or investment company can accept deposits worth up to one and a half times its net owned funds.

Fixed Deposit.question: Can an NRI invest in deposits?
Fixed Deposit.answer: Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) can invest in fixed deposits with public limited companies in India under certain conditions. For instance, if the Indian company has already obtained permission to accept deposits from NRIs, the investor can invest in such a deposit without seeking separate permission.

MTF.question: What are the eligibility requirements for Members to provide Margin Trading Facility (MTF) to clients?
MTF.answer: Only a corporate Member with a net worth of at least Rs.3.00 crores, computed as per the Dr. L C Gupta formula, shall be eligible to offer margin trading facility to their clients after obtaining prior approval of the Exchange.

MTF.question: How to obtain permission from the Exchange to offer margin trading facility to clients?
MTF.answer: Member needs to seek prior permission of the Exchange by submitting an application form under ENIT New Compliance on Member’s Portal as per the format mentioned at the link- https://www.nseindia.com/trade/Membership-formats under the tab “Form for applying for approval or withdrawal of Margin Trading Facility” along with a certified extract of Board Resolution and Auditor’s certificate on net worth.

MTF.question: How to apply for withdrawal of MTF?
MTF.answer: Member can apply for withdrawal by submitting an online application form under ENIT New Compliance on Member’s Portal as per the format mentioned at the link- https://www.nseindia.com/trade/Membership-formats under the tab “Form for applying for approval or withdrawal of Margin Trading Facility” along with a certified extract of Board Resolution. While seeking the withdrawal, the Member must ensure that all open positions under MTF are liquidated and reported to the Exchange accordingly.

MTF.question: Is consent from the clients required before offering Margin Trading facility to them?
MTF.answer: Member shall obtain client consent in writing in their own hand or any irrefutable electronic method. Rights & obligations and the terms and conditions shall also be communicated to the client.

MTF.question: Does Member require to allot a single UCC to the same client who avails both Normal Trading and Margin Trading Facility?
MTF.answer: All clients shall have only one unique client code (UCC) per Member which shall be reported to the Exchange. Clients availing Margin Trading Facility (MTF) should be segregated in the back office with the Trading Member, namely MTF account & Non-MTF account, which shall be mapped to the said unique client code (UCC).

MTF.question: Which securities are eligible for margin trading?
MTF.answer: All the equity shares classified as 'Group I security' as per Section 1.1.3 of Chapter IV of SEBI Master Circular No. SEBI/HO/MRD/DP/CIR/P/117 dated October 25, 2019 (previous SEBI circular ref. no. SEBI/HO/MRD/DP/CIR/P/2016/135 dated December 16, 2016) are eligible for margin trading facility.

Commodities.question: What is Commodity Market?
Commodities.answer: Commodity Market is a place wherein trades can trade in a wide variety of Commodities ranging from agricultural products to energy resources and precious metals. It is a derivatives trading market, i.e., traders buy and sell Commodities with the intention of booking profits by entering into Futures and Options trading contracts.

Commodities.question: What is Commodities Trading?
Commodities.answer: Commodity trading can be defined as the process of buying and selling a range of raw materials or products – from agricultural to energy resources, crude oils, precious metal bullion, etc. This trade takes place through futures and options derivative contracts, wherein the Commodity derives its value from an underlying asset – the Commodity being traded. Commodities are usually traded in lots, and sellers can take physical delivery of the products traded or settle the trade in cash.

Commodities.question: How to open a Commodity trading online account in India?
Commodities.answer: You can open a Commodity trading online account with a SEBI-recognised brokerage firm in India. You only need to fill an online form and submit the necessary KYC documents – PAN, ID, Address proof documents, and your income proof documents. You also need to submit your DEMAT account details so that the broker can link your Commodity trading account to the DEMAT account. Linking the two accounts enables the broker to debit or credit your derivatives. Once you provide these details, you can pay the account activation fee, and the broker opens your account. You will receive your Trading ID and password, and you can begin Commodity trading.

Commodities.question: Is it mandatory to provide income proof documents to activate a Commodity trading account?
answer: Yes, Commodity trading is classified as derivatives trading. Per SEBI guidelines, if you wish to conduct derivatives trading, you must submit your income proof documents. The list of valid income proof documents include,
A copy of your Bank Account Statement for the last 3 months
A copy of your DEMAT account holding statement
A copy of Form 16
Latest Salary Slip
Copy of duly filed Tax Returns
Net Worth Certificate
Copy of Annual Accounts.

Commodities.question: The Commodity market in India is regulated by which entity?
Commodities.answer: Up until Sep 2015, Forward Market Commission (FMC) was the Commodity market regulator in India. This commission was governed by the Ministry of Consumer Affairs. However, since Sep 2015, the Securities and Exchange Board of India has become the sole Commodity market regulator in the country ensuring fair trading. The Commodity Derivatives Market Regulation Department (CDMRD) is appointed to carry out the daily operations under SEBI's rules and guidelines.

Commodities.question: What are the different Commodities traded in India?
Commodities.answer: Commodity Trading in India involves several agricultural Commodities and natural resources. Agricultural Commodities are known as soft Commodities, whereas natural resources that are mined or extracted are known as hard Commodities. The different types of Commodities traded in India include Gold and Silver Bullion, Base Metals, Energy Resources, Spices, Agricultural Commodities, Oils, Food Grains, etc. Please note that all Commodities are not traded on every Exchange.

Commodities.question: What are Commodity futures and options?
Commodities.answer: Commodity market traders use Futures and options Commodity trading contracts. Traders who enter into futures contracts agree to buy or sell Commodities on a fixed, future date, at a fixed price, irrespective of the Commodity's market price on the contract maturity date. Traders can also enter into options contracts with more or less the same conditions as the futures contract. However, Commodities options traders have the right or option to exit the trade if it does not prove profitable. Such traders must pay a margin while entering into the contract and forfeit the margin if they choose to exit the contract.

Commodities.question: How is Commodity trading beneficial?
Commodities.answer: Commodity trading proves beneficial in several ways. Firstly, traders benefit from the inflation of Commodity prices. During inflation, the cost of Commodities and raw materials increase substantially, which helps Commodity traders book maximum profits. Traders can also hedge their Commodity portfolios against a declining USD and save their assets. Furthermore, they can take advantage of various socio-economic and geopolitical events and uncertain global and economic periods, during which time the supply of goods is constricted. They can speculate or hedge their trades and book significant profits resulting from shortages.

Commodities.question: What are the benefits of Commodity market trading?
answer: Traders can derive the following benefits of Commodity trading:
Commodity trading allows investors to diversify their investment portfolios by adding a different asset class to them.

Commodities assist traders in hedging their investments against inflation since the prices of Commodities typically rise during high inflation periods. Hedging allows investors to maintain their purchasing power parity.

Geopolitical events like natural disasters, economic crisis, wars, etc., lead to scarcity of Commodities, and by extension, increased prices. Trading Commodities allow traders to guard their losses by leveraging price swings strategically.

Commodities.question: What are the different types of Commodity trading products in India?
answer: The different types of Commodity trading products in India primarily comprise the following categories:
Agricultural Commodities like pulses, rice, cotton, rubber, legumes, etc.
Metallic Commodities and industrial metals like gold and silver coins, bullion, bars, aluminium, lead, copper, etc.
Energy resources like coal, crude oil, natural gas, etc.

Commodities.question: On what exchange does commodity trading occur, and what are the timings?
answer: In India, commodity trading takes place on 6 exchanges, namely
Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX)
National Multi Commodity Exchange (NMCE)
Indian Commodity Exchange (ICEX)
National Commodity and Derivatives Exchange (NCDEX)
The Universal Commodity Exchange (UCX)
Ace Derivatives Exchange (ACE)

The commodity market timings depend on the type of commodity being traded. You can trade in agricultural commodities from IST 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM from Monday to Friday. The trade timings for non-agricultural commodities are IST 10:00 AM to 11:30 PM during summers and IST 10:00 AM to 11:55 PM during winters from Monday to Friday.

Commodities.question: Which are the Commodity Trading exchanges in India?
answer: Commodity trading happens in dedicated exchanges created for this purpose. Some of the prominent ones in India include:

The National Commodity & Derivatives Exchange Limited (NCDEX) - for agricultural pulses, products such as grains (flour, barley, maize, paddy) seeds, soybean, oil, cotton, spices (sugar, black and white pepper, cumin, turmeric, coriander, anise, cardamom, cinnamon) etc.

The Multi Commodity Exchange of India Limited (MCX) - for billions of precious metals like gold or silver coins, guineas, bars, base metals such as aluminium, lead, zinc, nickel, energy sources like crude oil, natural gas, agricultural items such as rubber, palm oil, cotton, exotic spices, etc.
The Indian Commodity Exchange Limited (ICEX) - for most of the agricultural products as listed above, fiber and jute, and diamonds and steel as well.

Commodities.question: Who are the different types of participants involved in Commodity trading in India?
Commodities.answer: Commodity traders in India are primarily categorised into two types – speculators and hedgers. Speculators, also known as intra-day traders, typically try to exit their long or short positions on the same trading day or after a few days. These traders are known to be direction-agnostic, i.e., they leverage their trades as per market momentum by relying on technical charts to base their trades. On the other hand, hedgers take an offsetting or contrary position in their investments to balance their gains and losses from an underlying asset. They take on an opposite position that allows them to protect themselves against price movements and cover all their bases to ensure they take the minimum risk on their investments.

Commodities.question: What is the settlement process in Commodity trading?
answer: Settlement in Commodity trading involves tallying raised and pending buy and sell instructions, and subsequently transferring the ownership of said Commodities from the seller to the buyer in lieu of the funds exchanged. The Commodity trading settlement process includes the following steps
The relevant trade details are sent to the clearing corporations by the applicable Commodity exchanges
Thereafter, the clearing corporation informs the buyers and sellers regarding their obligations and pay-in advice on funds
The underlying clearing banks are sent a communication to ensure that they make funds available by the pay-in time
The clearing banks debit the account of the clearing member and credit the same amount to the account of the clearing corporation to complete the pay-in of funds for executed trades
In the case of pay-out of funds, the clearing bank credits the account of clearing member upon the instructions of the clearing corporation

Commodities.question: Is physical delivery of Commodities a mandatory requirement?
answer: Yes, SEBI has made mandatory for taking or giving delivery of Commodities upon the completion of the trade.
There are three types of delivery mechanisms that are prevalent in the Commodity market. These are compulsory delivery, sellers’ option, and intention matching.
In compulsory delivery, all open positions, upon expiry of contracts, need to be settled physically.
In the sellers’ option, sellers will have an edge while selecting the delivery location and quantity.
In intention matching, physical delivery of Commodities happens only when both buyer and seller agree to exchange the physical Commodity.
Traders are advised to check and understand the applicable delivery mechanism before going ahead with the trade.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: What is Gold ETF?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: Gold Exchange Traded Fund is a commodity-based Mutual Fund that invests in assets like gold. These exchange-traded funds perform like individual stocks and are traded in stock exchanges similar to trading in stocks.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: How Does Gold ETF Work?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: A gold ETF holds gold assets and is traded on a stock exchange. Its price is directly linked to the price of gold. So, if the gold price goes up by 2%, the ETF value will likely go up by about 2%. Similarly, if the gold price goes down, the ETF value will also decrease.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: What is Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB)? Who is the issuer?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: SGBs are government securities denominated in grams of gold. They are substitutes for holding physical gold. Investors have to pay the issue price in cash and the bonds will be redeemed in cash on maturity. The Bond is issued by Reserve Bank on behalf of Government of India.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: Who is eligible to invest in the SGBs?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: Persons resident in India as defined under Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 are eligible to invest in SGB. Eligible investors include individuals, HUFs, trusts, universities and charitable institutions. Individual investors with subsequent change in residential status from resident to non-resident may continue to hold SGB till early redemption/maturity.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: Who Should Invest in Gold ETF?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: People who want to trade gold without owning it can invest in gold ETFs to potentially increase their income.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: What is Gold ETF Taxation?
answer: Gold ETFs are subject to two types of taxes, as announced in the recent Budget:
a. Long-term capital gains (LTCG) will be uniformly taxed at 12.5% for all classes of assets. The indexation benefit that investors used to get was removed in Budget 2024.
b. Short-term capital gains from stocks, equity funds and units of business trust (InvIT and REIT) will be taxed at 20% as against 15% earlier.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: Can a Minor invest in SGB?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: Yes. The application on behalf of the minor has to be made by his/her guardian.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: What is the minimum and maximum limit for investment?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: The Bonds are issued in denominations of one gram of gold and in multiples thereof. Minimum investment in the Bond shall be one gram with a maximum limit of subscription of 4 kg for individuals, 4 kg for Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) and 20 kg for trusts and similar entities notified by the government from time to time per fiscal year (April – March). In case of joint holding, the limit applies to the first applicant. The annual ceiling will include bonds subscribed under different tranches during initial issuance by Government and those purchased from the secondary market. The ceiling on investment will not include the holdings as collateral by banks and other Financial Institutions.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: Can each member of my family buy 4Kg in their own name?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: Yes, each family member can buy the bonds in his/her own name if they satisfy the eligibility criteria as defined at Q No.2.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: Can an investor/trust buy 4 Kg/20 Kg worth of SGB every year?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: Yes. An investor/trust can buy 4 Kg/20 Kg worth of gold every year as the ceiling has been fixed on a fiscal year (April-March) basis.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: Is the maximum limit of 4 Kg applicable in case of joint holding?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: The maximum limit will be applicable to the first applicant in case of a joint holding for that specific application.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: What is the rate of interest and how will the interest be paid?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: The Bonds bear interest at the rate of 2.50 per cent (fixed rate) per annum on the amount of initial investment. Interest will be credited semi-annually to the bank account of the investor and the last interest will be payable on maturity along with the principal.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: What will I get on redemption?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: On maturity, the Gold Bonds shall be redeemed in Indian Rupees and the redemption price shall be based on simple average of closing price of gold of 999 purity of previous 3 business days from the date of repayment, published by the India Bullion and Jewelers Association Limited.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: Can I encash the bond anytime I want? Is premature redemption allowed?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: Though the tenor of the bond is 8 years, early encashment/redemption of the bond is allowed after fifth year from the date of issue on coupon payment dates. The bond will be tradable on Exchanges, if held in demat form. It can also be transferred to any other eligible investor.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: What do I have to do if I want to exit my investment?
Gold ETF/Bond.answer: In case of premature redemption, investors can approach the concerned bank/SHCIL offices/Post Office/agent thirty days before the coupon payment date. Request for premature redemption can only be entertained if the investor approaches the concerned bank/post office at least one day before the coupon payment date. The proceeds will be credited to the customer’s bank account provided at the time of applying for the bond.

Gold ETF/Bond.question: Can I get the bonds in demat form?
answer: Yes. The bonds can be held in demat account. A specific request for the same must be made in the application form itself.
Till the process of dematerialization is completed, the bonds will be held in RBI’s books. The facility for conversion to demat will also be available subsequent to allotment of the bond.

IPO.question: What is Book Building?
IPO.answer: SEBI Guidelines defines Book Building as a process undertaken by which a demand for the securities proposed to be issued by a corporate body is elicited and built up and the price for such securities is assessed for the determination of the quantum of such securities to be issued by means of a notice, circular, advertisement, document or information memoranda or offer document.

IPO.question: What is the main difference between offer of shares through book building and offer of shares through normal public issue?
IPO.answer: Price at which securities will be allotted is not known in case of offer of shares through book building while in case of offer of shares through normal public issue, price is known in advance to investor. In case of Book Building, the demand can be known everyday as the book is built. But in case of the public issue the demand is known at the close of the issue.

IPO.question: What is minimum number of days for which bid should remain open in book building?
IPO.answer: Book should remain open for minimum of 3 working days.

IPO.question: When can be the BIDS towards IPO be Collected?
IPO.answer: "SEBI ICDR Regulation 2018 – 46" & 266 (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) provides an IPO Subscription Period during which the bids shall be accepted by the bidding system. Any blocking of funds towards IPO as per the applicable circular prior to the subscription period shall not be permitted. All members are advised to adhere to the same and other guidelines issued by SEBI/ NSE time to time.

IPO.question: Can open outcry system be used for book building?
IPO.answer: No. As per SEBI, only electronically linked transparent facility is allowed to be used in case of book building.

IPO.question: Is the issue price for placement portion and net offer to public the same?
IPO.answer: Yes.

IPO.question: What is the floor price in case of book building?
IPO.answer: Floor price is the minimum price at which bids can be made.

IPO.question: Can the Individual Investor use book building facility for making an application?
IPO.answer: Yes.

IPO.question: What are the formalities to be completed by members before participating in an IPO?
IPO.answer: The member has to submit a one-time undertaking in prescribed format to the membership department. Members have to make the request in prescribed format giving details of the user ids along with the VSAT numbers.

IPO.question: Can more than one issue be held simultaneously using NSE IPO system?
IPO.answer: Yes.

IPO.question: Can the bidder revise his bids?
IPO.answer: Yes.

IPO.question: Which members will be allowed to participate in book building of issue?
IPO.answer: Book Running Lead Manager appointed by the issuer will intimate to the exchange the list of members who are eligible to participate in the issue. These members will be allowed to enter the bids in the IPO.

IPO.question: How to install the IPO software in trading terminal?
IPO.answer: The members have to copy the NTIPOTDR.EXE file in root directory and then inflate the file by using command NTIPOTDR -D -O. The above executable file is available in extranet under common/ipo directory.

IPO.question: What proof can bidder request from trading member for entering bids?
IPO.answer: Whenever a bid is entered by trading members in to the system, a unique transaction registration slip is automatically generated. Transaction registration slip gives details regarding number of shares bid for, price, the client name etc.

IPO.question: Is it possible to enter bids less than floor price?
IPO.answer: No. The system automatically rejects the bids if price is less than floor price.

IPO.question: Is the Margin amount a compulsory field in order entry?
IPO.answer: Margin amount is a compulsory numeric field. However if member collects no margin amount then he has to enter 0 in margin amount field.

IPO.question: Can members set up order limits for themselves?
IPO.answer: Corporate Manager of each member can set up order limits for all the dealers under him.

IPO.question: Will the member be able to see the bid details for all the issues?
IPO.answer: User will see the bid details for all the issues. However, he is able to enter bids only for those issues for which he has been appointed as member by Book Running Lead Manager.

IPO.question: Can the Syndicate Members set limits on orders to be entered by other members?
IPO.answer: No. However the Syndicate Members can see the orders entered by members who are activated under him, by downloading EOD report.

IPO.question: Can the Syndicate members modify or cancel the orders of other members?
IPO.answer: Syndicate members cannot cancel or modify the bids entered by other members.

IPO.question: What are the reports downloaded to Syndicate members?
IPO.answer: Syndicate Member (SM) will get a report download of his own orders as well as of all other members who are activated under him.

IPO.question: Will the change in the user level hierarchy in the IPO segment affect any change in the Capital Market Segment or vice versa?
IPO.answer: The environment for Capital Market Segment and IPO book building segment are different. It is a matter of convenience, that User Ids available in the Capital Market Segment are used in IPO book building segment. Hence, any change in either of the Market segment will not affect the other market.

Bonds.question: What are bonds?
Bonds.answer: Bonds are fixed-income securities in which you provide funds to a government, organization, or company with the expectation of stable payments of interest and your principal back at maturity.

Bonds.question: How do I purchase bonds online with Integrated?
Bonds.answer: Through the secure platform of Integrated, you will find available bonds through browsing, select the bonds according to your investment goals, and purchase them with professional support at every stage.

Bonds.question: Which types of bonds are provided in the Indian market?
Bonds.answer: Typical ones are government bonds, corporate bonds, tax-saving bonds, and capital gain bonds, which have varying safety and returns along with tax benefits.

Bonds.question: Are government bonds of India safe?
Bonds.answer: Government bonds of India are said to be among the least risky investments since the bonds are guaranteed by the Government of India and therefore they are suitable for conservative investors.

Bonds.question: What is the meaning of tax-saving bonds, and who qualifies to invest in them?
Bonds.answer: Tax savings bonds, including some capital gain bonds, will enable you to pay less tax and enjoy fixed returns, which makes them perfect for investors interested in searching for both income and tax-efficient bonds.

Bonds.question: What is the difference between government bonds and corporate bonds as investment options?
Bonds.answer: Corporate bonds in general have higher interest rates than government bonds and have different levels of credit risk concerning the entity releasing the bond based on its financial standing.

Bonds.question: Is there a fixed return in bonds?
Bonds.answer: The majority of bonds are fixed interest rate and thus predictable, although some of them could incorporate floating rates that are pegged to market indices. Integrated guides you in making decisions that are effective for your goals.

Bonds.question: What are some of the uses of bonds when forming a fixed-income portfolio?
Bonds.answer: Bonds are the main components of a fixed-income portfolio due to their stability, steady payment of interest periodically, and also because they provide diversification to decrease the risk of the portfolio.

GlobalAccess.question: What is Integrated Global Investments IFSC Pvt. Ltd.?
GlobalAccess.answer: Integrated Global Investments IFSC Pvt. Ltd. (Fully owned subsidiary for Integrated Enterprises (India) Private Limited) is an entity established at GIFT IFSC to provide regulated, compliant and professionally guided access to global investment opportunities, particularly U.S.-listed securities at present, under the IFSCA framework.

GlobalAccess.question: Who are the target clients for this offering?
answer: The offering is designed for:
Resident Indian individuals
NRIs & OCIs (excluding those residing in the U.S.)
High-Net-Worth Investors
Foreign non-U.S. clients
All clients are onboarded strictly as per IFSCA, FEMA, KYC and AML norms.

GlobalAccess.question: What is India INX Global Access (GA)?
GlobalAccess.answer:
India INX Global Access is a GIFT City–based regulated platform that allows eligible investors to invest directly in U.S.-listed stocks through a fully digital and compliant framework.

Integrated is LIVE on the India INX GA platform, enabling seamless global investing.

GlobalAccess.question: Which markets and instruments can investors access?
answer: Investors can access:
U.S.-listed equities
Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs)
Fractional shares
Select fixed income instruments (where permitted)

GlobalAccess.question: Is the onboarding process digital?
answer: Yes. The onboarding is fully digital, including:
KYC & AML verification
Documentation as per IFSCA norms
Account activation typically within T+2 working days

GlobalAccess.question: Who is eligible to open an account under this platform at present?
answer: Eligible investors at present:
Indian residents
Foreign non-U.S. clients
Not eligible at present:
U.S. citizens
NRIs residing in the U.S.

GlobalAccess.question: What documents are required for account opening?
answer: PAN
Address proof (Aadhaar for Indian residents)
ID & address proof for foreign non-U.S. clients
Additional bank-related documentation may apply for remittances.

GlobalAccess.question: How are investments funded?
answer: All investments are routed through the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS):
Annual limit: USD 250,000 per individual
Funds must be maintained in USD

GlobalAccess.question: Is Form A2 required for remittance?
answer: ICICI, HDFC, Axis Bank clients: No Form A2 required (processed through online arrangements)
Other banks: Currency conversion + Form A2 submission required
Funds are typically credited to the trading account within 48 working hours.

GlobalAccess.question: How does trading take place?
answer: Orders are placed directly through a dedicated trading link
Trading happens during U.S. market hours
Investors are fully responsible for order accuracy
No error trades are permitted

GlobalAccess.question: Where are the shares held?
GlobalAccess.answer: Shares are held with the foreign broker (ViewTrade Securities Inc.) under the India INX Global Access IFSC Limited framework.

GlobalAccess.question: What investor protection measures are available?
GlobalAccess.answer:
Foreign broker accounts are insured up to USD 500,000 per Account (fractional units excluded).

Corporate actions (dividends, bonus, rights) are credited to the broker first and then transferred to the investor.

GlobalAccess.question: How are dividends taxed?
answer: U.S. dividends attract 25% withholding tax
Dividends are taxable in India at the applicable slab rate
Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) can be claimed by filing Form 67

GlobalAccess.question: What is the capital gains tax treatment in India?
answer: Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG):
Holding period: More than 12 months
Tax rate: 12.5% flat (no indexation)
Short-Term Capital Gains (STCG):
Tax rate: 20% flat (effective from 23/07/2024)
Note: No LTCG exemption of ₹1.25 lakh is available for foreign stocks.

GlobalAccess.question: Is capital gains tax payable in the U.S.?
GlobalAccess.answer: No. For Indian residents, capital gains tax is not applicable in the U.S. due to DTAA provisions.

GlobalAccess.question: Are there any reporting requirements in India?
answer: Yes. Investors must:
Declare foreign assets and income in the Indian Income Tax Return (ITR)
Maintain broker statements, dividend statements and contract notes
Convert foreign income into INR using RBI TT rates

GlobalAccess.question: Does Integrated provide advisory or research services?
GlobalAccess.answer: Integrated facilitates integration with research and advisory ecosystems, where permitted under applicable regulations. Services are offered strictly within regulatory boundaries.

GlobalAccess.question: What makes Integrated different from other global investing options?
answer: Strong compliance and operational framework
Decades of capital market experience
Dedicated support for NRI and cross-border investors
Single-window, transparent and regulator-aligned process
No hidden costs or undisclosed risks

GlobalAccess.question: How can investors open an account?
answer: Accounts can be opened through:
The India INX GA portal (dedicated member link)
The Integrated website under Login / Register

GlobalAccess.question: Where can investors get support or assistance?
GlobalAccess.answer: Integrated provides a dedicated GIFT City Help Desk for onboarding, compliance and operational support. Investors may contact Mr.Ganesh on +91 8925801947 or write to giftcity@integratedindia.in or ganesh@integratedindia.in.

GlobalAccess.question: How to close my Global Trading Account?
answer: You can close your Global trading account by sending us an email request from registered email id to giftcity@integratedindia.in or ganesh@integratedindia.in. The closure process takes 2 working days once you submit your request. Before closing your account:
Clear any negative balance in account
Sell off any holdings in the account
Withdraw any cash balance from the account
Download all necessary reports (trade confirms, ledger, and P&L statements), as you cannot access these reports once your account closes
In case you wish to move securities to another broker, please transfer shares and cash and then request account closure.

Please note that upon submission of an account closure request, the client irrevocably agrees that any residual amounts, including but not limited to dividends, corporate action proceeds or any other entitlements arising from prior holdings and received post-closure, shall not be credited to the client’s account. The client acknowledges and accepts that such amounts may be forfeited and that no claims shall lie against the Company in respect of the same.
